title: SQL Editor
text: SQL Editor Use the SQL Editor to send ad hoc queries to the database. This is only supported by databases that include a Data Manipulation Language (DML). To open this tool, choose Main Menu ( ≡ ) > Edit SQL . You can connect to databases that support SQL, and to some others to a limited extent. For more information on the extent of support, see the DBMS connector documentation for your DBMS. Figure 1. SQL Workbench Fields Data Path —DBMS or network path used to access the data. If you enter an incorrect path assignment on this form, an implicit and immediate apexit is generated; that is, you exit the application immediately. SQL Command —SQL command to execute. Do not use a semicolon when entering the command. SQL statements that exceed 2 kilobytes are truncated. DBMS Output —query results. How to Querying a Database Related Links SQL and Stored Procedures Support For SQL-enabled databases, you can use the sql ProcScript instruction and SQL Editor to send SQL instructions to the database. Some databases also support stored procedures. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
